comments.body: I seem to recall -- and this could be outdated -- that several of the libraries decided to be "helpful" and use the system defined line ending. It might be possible to set the DefaultIndenter to a specific line feed. See, perhaps, Configure line endings.
call setDefaultPrettyPrinter with an instance of PrettyPrinter that does what you want.

answers.body:
Your example uses ObjectMapper.readTree(), which returns an instance of JsonNode and its method JsonNode.toPrettyString() is not configurable.

To configure line endings you can serialize with ObjectMapper, which is configurable. You could do something like this:

var prettyPrinter = new DefaultPrettyPrinter()
    .withObjectIndenter(new DefaultIndenter().withLinefeed("\n"));
var objectMapper = new ObjectMapper()
    .setDefaultPrettyPrinter(prettyPrinter)
    .enable(SerializationFeature.INDENT_OUTPUT);
var jsonNode = objectMapper.readTree("{\"key\":\"value\"}");

return objectMapper.writeValueAsString(jsonNode);

Or alternatively with ObjectMapper.writerWithDefaultPrettyPrinter() (still needing an instance of Indenter to configure line endings):

var prettyPrinter = new DefaultPrettyPrinter()
    .withObjectIndenter(new DefaultIndenter().withLinefeed("\n"));
var objectMapper = new ObjectMapper()
    .setDefaultPrettyPrinter(prettyPrinter);
var jsonNode = objectMapper.readTree("{\"key\":\"value\"}");

return objectMapper.writerWithDefaultPrettyPrinter().writeValueAsString(jsonNode);

You could also potentially solve your issue with System.setProperty("line.separator", "\n");, but it would have to happen before ObjectMapper and connected classes are loaded from class path. I wouldn't consider this a recommended approach, but given your simple example it could work:

System.setProperty("line.separator", "\n");
var objectMapper = new ObjectMapper();
return objectMapper.readTree("{\"key\":\"value\"}").toPrettyString();

title: fasterxml.jackson ObjectMapper's prettyPrint uses \r\n instead of \n
body:
When running on windows I always get "\r\n" in the output, but I was trying to just return "\n"

    ObjectMapper objectMapper = new ObjectMapper();
return objectMapper.readTree("{\"key\":\"value\"}")
    .toPrettyString();

Is there a easy way to achieve this?
